name: Welcome To Comeketo — Rodrigo's Deck (Founder Voice & Core Sales Architecture)
subject: Rodrigo's working sales deck — the founder-voice origin of Comeketo's tasting-day pitch. Source text shows the internal 'director's copy' with scratch notes still visible (brainstorm labels like 'Herding Cats: The Vendor Edition', 'The Vendor Tsunami', 'Three Paths, No Direction'). This is the most important brand-voice document in the raw data: it defines the 4-Secrets framework, the price-lock policy in operational language, the integrity promise, the objection-handling cadence, and the named partner bonus stack. The Wedding Experience deck (see knowledge/brand_wedding_experience.json) is a polished derivative of this.
source_files: Welcome To Comeketo Catering presentation Rodrigo Copy .txt

beats: 'Choosing the right caterer can make or break your event'
The couple's own to-do list (dozens of items)
Venue A (kickbacks) vs Venue B (trusted vendors)
Usually 50% of event investment is Food/Bev

structure.arc: Secret #4 — How To Evaluate A Caterer

beats: 'Trust. It's what matters in this business.'
7 confirmation questions that all want a yes
Choice 2: Comeketo Auto-Pilot (explicitly mentions 'Brazilian Grill' as a proof point)
Free Added Protein ($650 value, requires Deluxe Churrasco+)
Venue note: 'THIS VERY VENUE — Officially Opens this Spring! 50-100 People — Hall+Catering+DJ options'
Price Lock Guarantee policy in explicit language
'We want to check out more caterers'
DJ Smokey: $495 package → escalates to $1,000 premium upgrades (Photo Booth / Monogram / Cold Spark)

deck_architecture.structural_observation: Deck is internal/director's-copy — many slides carry leftover scratch labels ('Herding Cats: The Vendor Edition', 'Simplifying the Vendor Tsunami', 'Stuck in the frame of thinking of point', 'Three Paths, No Direction', 'The Supplier Synchronization Struggle'). These are brainstorm residue, not audience copy. The cleaner Wedding Experience deck is what actually gets presented.

four_secrets_framework.framing_setup: 4 secrets catering companies don't want you to know

secret_1.name: The Partnership Pile-Up
secret_1.subtitle: Coordination Gone Wild
secret_1.problem_statement: Couples have dozens of decisions already on their plate (venue, officiant, photographer, DJ, band, lighting, dress, rings, registry, cake, rehearsal, vows, pack bags, thank you notes, etc.). Then each vendor itself has sub-decisions (menu customization, tasting, delivery, staffing, post-event cleanup / drink menu, bartenders, glassware, liquor permits / tablecloths, napkins, dinnerware). Nobody talks about the 'inter-vendor issue' — coordinating all of them.
secret_1.comeketo_answer: We are 'The Sweet Spot' — we handle the pile-up for you.

secret_2.name: Most Caterers Do Not Price Lock Their Quote
secret_2.subtitle: How can you be sure today's catering prices won't double tomorrow?
secret_2.problem_statement: Prices (food costs, labor costs) are volatile. Most caterers requote at book-time. The 'eggs' metaphor opens the slide.
secret_2.comeketo_answer: 2-Year Price-Lock Guarantee — today's quote is locked in up to 2 years, even on a minimum package, and follows the couple to any venue, any guest count, any date within the next 2 years.

secret_3.name: Most Caterers Pay to be a Preferred Vendor, Give Kickbacks, and Inflate Prices
secret_3.subtitle: Venue Deals Aren't Always About You / Integrity vs Pay to Win
secret_3.problem_statement: Preferred-vendor kickbacks inflate prices. The couple pays more and gets worse options. 'This practice is rampant.'
secret_3.comeketo_answer: Venue B model: builds trusted vendors, more affordable, stronger relationships, prioritizes integrity. Payoff: 'Kickback Free = Superior Vendor Network.'

secret_4.name: How To Evaluate A Caterer
secret_4.subtitle: The Biggest Secret
secret_4.problem_statement: Couples don't know what to evaluate on.

professionalism_checklist: Do they not only respond promptly but also provide relevant solutions to your concerns?
Do they demonstrate capability by accommodating your unique needs and dietary restrictions?
Do they have a clear step-by-step process?
Do they hold the necessary licenses, certifications, and insurance?
Do they follow up with you proactively about next steps and confirmations?
Do they honor timelines for decisions and updates?
Do they communicate what the server-to-guest ratio is?
Do they have a network of vendors in the wedding sphere?
Do they have transparency on what they can and can't do?

secret_4.rodbot_note: This checklist is self-flattering — Comeketo's answer is 'yes' to all 9. It's a diagnostic tool that doubles as a scorecard where we pass and competitors fail. Rodbot should treat this as the quality bar — any Comeketo-branded reply ought to clear all 9.

price_lock_policy_operational.stated_terms: All prices on your quote here today, with Comeketo Catering — even if you book only on a minimum package — are price locked for up to 2 years. Guaranteed.
price_lock_policy_operational.portability: Price lock follows you to any venue, any guest count, and any date within the next 2 years.
price_lock_policy_operational.quote_validity: All quoted prices are valid for 14 days.
price_lock_policy_operational.minimum_package_qualifier: Lock-in available on a minimum package — couple can add more later.
rodbot_implications: This is operationally specific enough to quote in Rodbot replies verbatim: '2-year price lock, follows you to any venue/guest count/date, available even on a minimum package.'
14-day quote validity is a hard-edged closing window — Rodbot should track quote dates and surface the deadline, not let it silently lapse.
Cross-reference with reality: confirm Ops actually honors 2-year locks on minimum packages. If there are exceptions, the deck copy is a liability.

integrity_promise_operational.stated_terms: We refer partners because they are the best, not because they pay us. No kickbacks, no hidden fees.
integrity_promise_operational.venue_archetype_frame: Venue A creates kickbacks → higher costs, who's best interest? Venue B builds trusted vendors → more affordable, stronger relationships, prioritizes integrity.
rodbot_implications: CRITICAL: This claim is the single highest-risk brand claim in the corpus. The Partnership Program corpus (78KB .md + 338KB .txt) must be read and reconciled before Rodbot ever repeats the 'no kickbacks' line. If the partnership program includes any referral compensation, the copy is misleading and needs to be reworked to something defensible.
Either way, Rodbot's rule: do not extend this claim beyond what the Partnership Program doc explicitly supports.

objection_rebuttals.objection: We still want to check out a few more caterers.
objection_rebuttals.opening_acknowledgment: I get it. It's a big commitment, and you want to be absolutely certain.
rebuttal_bullets: Not all caterers are equal — some shine in tastings but struggle with large-scale service.
You still have 30 other things to plan.
The longer you wait, the higher your chance your preferred date books up.
objection_rebuttals.underlying_move: Validate first ('I get it'), then use decision-fatigue + scarcity + execution-risk as the three closing levers.

objection_rebuttals.objection: We need to think about our budget more before deciding.
objection_rebuttals.opening_acknowledgment: I hear you. Budgets are crucial for any event.
rebuttal_bullets: We are the only caterer that offers a price lock guarantee.
This allows you to secure your date on a minimum package, and add more later.
Protects your date, guarantees your pricing.
All quoted prices are valid for 14 days.
objection_rebuttals.underlying_move: Reframe budget concern as resolved by price lock — flip a 'wait' into a 'book minimum now.' Quote-validity tail is the hard close.

objection_rebuttals.objection: We don't have a final date (or final headcount) yet, so we can't commit.
objection_rebuttals.opening_acknowledgment: I get it. Nailing down a precise date and guest count is tough.
rebuttal_bullets: You can always add more guests later.
Your price lock follows you to any venue, any guest count, and any date within the next 2 years.
objection_rebuttals.underlying_move: Remove the commitment barrier — the deposit locks pricing not logistics.

yes_ladder_close.framing: Before You Make the 2nd Biggest Decision for Your One-Big-Day…
questions: Do you believe that hiring the wrong caterer can make a great event go bad?
Do you want your catering to be one of the highlights of your one-big-day?
Would you love extra perks that enhance your event?
Do you want to feel more confident and stress free for your one-big-day?
Is it clear that this process can be simpler if you have the right partner?
Are you beginning to see how Comeketo can deliver that experience?
Would it be helpful to lock in a plan that guarantees peace of mind?
yes_ladder_close.underlying_move: Classic 7-step yes-chain — each question is near-impossible to answer 'no' to, and by the last one the prospect has verbally agreed to the entire pitch premise.
yes_ladder_close.rodbot_use: Rodbot should NOT mechanically deploy this ladder in conversation — it reads as manipulative in 1:1 chat. But it IS a useful internal checklist to verify every Comeketo reply frames the prospect's emotional stakes before pitching.

two_path_close.choice_1: You can steal the secrets, do all the research, call multiple caterers, and hope and pray you find the right fit.
two_path_close.choice_2: Or, you can put your catering on auto-pilot, and let us handle everything for you — leveraging our proven track record, Brazilian Grill, and added perks — so you can relax and know your event is in best hands.
two_path_close.rodbot_note: The 'Brazilian Grill' appears as a distinct product anchor in this close — not just 'catering.' This is the cultural/product specificity the Wedding Experience deck softens out. Rodbot voice should hold onto Brazilian/Churrasco as a concrete anchor rather than speaking in generic catering terms.

bonus_stack_operational.headline: Over $2,350+ in Bonuses, Incentives, Deals, and Savings

items.normally: $150/pp (appears to be a typo for flat $150 — Wedding Experience deck lists $150 flat)

items.qualifier: With any Deluxe Churrasco package or higher

offer_stated_multiple_ways: $1,000 Free Premium Upgrades (Photo Booth + Monogram + Cold Spark)
partner_bonuses.interpretation: The $495 figure on slide 60 is likely the baseline DJ package price — on slide 61+ it becomes $1,000 in free premium add-ons on top of booking. The $1,000 premium upgrade is what feeds the $2,350 total.

partner_bonuses.offer: 10% OR $300 off (stated as '$350+ in Savings' in the headline version)

bonus_stack_operational.math_reconciliation: $1,000 Comeketo + $1,000 DJ Smokey premium + $300-$700 photographer + $300+ decorator = $2,600-$3,000 range. Deck rounds to '$2,350+' to stay conservative/defensible. That's an accurate-ish floor.
rodbot_implications: These are NAMED partners with specific offers. They should become their own records when the Partnership Program corpus is indexed — potentially people/* or a partners/* kind.
Rodbot must not recommend an unnamed photographer/DJ/decorator if it's going to position the recommendation as 'our vetted partners' — the real list is short and specific.

venue_reveal_at_tasting.slide_48_copy: THIS VERY VENUE — Officially Opens this Spring! 50-100 People — Ask us about options for Hall+Catering+DJ.
venue_reveal_at_tasting.interpretation: Comeketo has (or is opening) its own venue. 50-100 capacity. Bundled Hall+Catering+DJ offer. This changes the pitch — Comeketo isn't only a caterer, it's a venue operator in its own right.
venue_reveal_at_tasting.rodbot_note: CRITICAL: this is a major business-model fact that doesn't show up in the other indexed material yet. Needs verification — is this venue live? Is it the tasting-room venue itself, or a separate hall? Cross-check with the Welcome deck's operational reality before Rodbot surfaces the offer.

credibility_anchors.750_plus_events: Decade of operation, 750+ events catered — same figure as Wedding Experience deck.
credibility_anchors.fox_news_2010: Featured on Fox News in 2010 — earliest datable credibility marker in the corpus. Anchors the '10+ years' claim.
credibility_anchors.awards: 'We've Won Awards' — award names not listed on this deck. Need to surface specifics from founder context.
testimonial_pulls: "Comeketo's Churrasco was absolutely one of the highlights of our wedding…"
"Throughout the years, Comeketo has consistently catered numerous events for us"
"They perfectly executed the seated dinner we had planned"

rodbot_voice_hooks.why_this_file_matters_for_rodbot: This is Rodrigo's own pitch in near-raw form. The Wedding Experience deck is a polished derivative. If we want Rodbot to sound like Rodrigo rather than a generic caterer's marketing voice, the distinctive moves live here: the 4 Secrets framing, the 'Sweet Spot' payoff, the 'This is your one-big-day' emotional frame, the 'Brazilian Grill' product anchor, the objection-handling cadence ('I get it / I hear you' → validate → reframe → close).
voice_signature_distinct_from_wedding_de: More direct (almost confrontational) — the word 'secret' and 'they don't tell you' run through the whole deck. This is an exposé voice, not just a marketing voice.
Product-specific anchor: Brazilian Grill / Churrasco — not generic catering.
Objection-handling cadence: validate first ('I get it', 'I hear you'), then provide a short bullet list of counter-points.
'One-big-day' as a compound noun — signature Rodrigo phrasing. Keep.
Repeats 'Trust. It's what matters in this business.' as a refrain across multiple slides.
do_use: Name problems competitors hide (pile-up, kickbacks, price volatility) before offering the Comeketo fix.
'I get it' / 'I hear you' opener before any rebuttal.
Brazilian Grill / Churrasco as the concrete product.
'One-big-day' as the stakes frame for weddings.
Reference the specific partner names (DJ Smokey, Above & Beyond Creatives, Ebenezer Decorations) when citing network depth.
do_not_use: The 7-question yes-ladder in conversational 1:1 replies — reads manipulative.
Vague 'award-winning' without a specific award name once we've confirmed one.
'No kickbacks' claim until Partnership Program corpus is reconciled.
The '50% of event investment goes to food/bev' claim as a pressure tactic — it's useful for scope-setting, not for closing.

copy_and_content_issues.where: Strings like 'Simplifying the Vendor Tsunami', 'Herding Cats: The Vendor Edition', 'Three Paths, No Direction', 'The Supplier Synchronization Struggle', 'Stuck in the frame of thinking of point' appear on many slide bodies.
copy_and_content_issues.fix: Internal-only file. Clean up before any external sharing.

copy_and_content_issues.issue: $150/pp vs $150 flat inconsistency on Mobile Bar.
copy_and_content_issues.where: Slide 45 says 'Normally: $150/pp' but then 'You save ($150)' — suggests it's actually a $150 flat setup, not $1.50/pp × 100. Wedding Experience deck lists it flat at $150.
copy_and_content_issues.fix: Correct the /pp on this deck to flat.

copy_and_content_issues.issue: Bonus total climbs across slides from $1,000 → $2,000+ → $2,350+.
copy_and_content_issues.fix: Pick one headline total and use it consistently. $2,350+ is the final stated figure.

copy_and_content_issues.issue: Title card says '4 secrets' but some slides leak '5 Secrets: Coming Up!' as residue.
copy_and_content_issues.fix: Was probably 5 at an earlier draft; consolidated to 4. Remove residue.

copy_and_content_issues.issue: Evaluation-checklist paragraph duplicated across many slides as body text.
copy_and_content_issues.where: The 'One of the first steps to evaluate a catering vendor is to check their references…' paragraph appears on slides 26, 27, 28, 29, 35, 36, 39, 40, 41, 43, 44, 45, 46, 48, 49, 50, 55, 56, 57, 58, 60, 62, 64, 65, 66, 67, 68.
copy_and_content_issues.fix: Almost certainly a 'default placeholder' that got left on template slides. Remove from everywhere except Step 1 slide.

cross_bedrock_dependencies.depends_on: Operations reality — does the team actually honor this on minimum packages?

cross_bedrock_dependencies.why: This claim is either correct or actively contradicted by the partnership program structure. Must reconcile.

cross_bedrock_dependencies.claim: Named partner offers (DJ Smokey / Above & Beyond / Ebenezer)
cross_bedrock_dependencies.why: These three names should become cross-referenceable records once the partnership corpus is indexed.
Surface this to Jake — we may be missing a whole business line in the bedrock.
Track whether the bonus stack is actually moving tasting→booking rate.

notes: This deck is labeled 'Rodrigo Copy' in the filename — it's the founder's working version, not the sales team's polished version.

Most slides have 'director's-copy' residue: strikethrough-worthy scratch notes that were never audience-facing. These are actually valuable for voice analysis because they show Rodrigo's thinking before it was polished.
The Wedding Experience deck (brand_wedding_experience.json) and this deck together form the core brand-voice corpus. The Sales Playbook v2.0 (still to be indexed) will be the third pillar.

sources.diff: Initial index from Welcome To Comeketo Catering presentation Rodrigo Copy .txt — 69 slides. 4-Secrets framework captured in full. Price-lock operational terms + integrity promise + 9-question evaluation checklist + 7-question yes-ladder + 3 objection rebuttals + bonus stack + 3 named partners (DJ Smokey / Above & Beyond / Ebenezer). Flagged Comeketo-owned-venue reveal on slide 48 as missing from bedrock. 5 copy issues flagged.
